title: AI vs ML vs Deep Learning вҖ” Untangled
key_insight: AI is the big idea. ML is one way to achieve it. Deep Learning is one type of ML that's currently winning.
body:
## Three Circles, Each Inside the Last

Think of three circles, like Russian nesting dolls:

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  AI  (any machine doing smart tasks)       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  ML  (learning from data)  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Deep Learning      в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (neural networks)  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### AI вҖ” The Big Umbrella

Any technique that makes a computer do something that seems 'intelligent'. This includes:
- Rule-based systems (if/else trees)
- Search algorithms (chess engines in the 1990s)
- Machine Learning
- And much more

### Machine Learning вҖ” Learning from Data

A specific approach to AI where the system learns from examples instead of following rules you write.

Instead of:
> Programmer writes rules вҶ’ Computer follows them

You get:
> Computer sees examples вҶ’ Computer figures out the rules

### Deep Learning вҖ” Neural Networks Specifically

A type of Machine Learning that uses artificial neural networks вҖ” layers of interconnected nodes inspired very loosely by the brain.

Deep Learning is why AI has been so impressive since 2012:
- It powered image recognition breakthroughs
- It's what runs ChatGPT and Claude
- It's what makes voice assistants work

### The Simple Version

| | What it is | Example |
|---|---|---|
| AI | Any smart software | Chess bot, spam filter, GPT |
| ML | Learns from data | Spam filter learning from email examples |
| Deep Learning | ML using neural networks | GPT-4, Stable Diffusion, AlphaFold |
common_mistakes: Using 'AI' and 'machine learning' as synonyms вҖ” ML is one approach to AI, not the whole field
Thinking deep learning is always the best tool вҖ” classical ML often wins on small, structured datasets
Assuming 'deep' = better вҖ” depth means more layers, not necessarily more accuracy
mini_exercise: For each of these, say which it is (AI, ML, or Deep Learning вҖ” pick the most specific): (1) A chess program from 1997 using minimax search. (2) A spam filter that trained on labelled emails. (3) GPT-4 generating text. (4) A thermostat that learns your preferred temperature.
real_world_usage: When Tesla says their self-driving uses 'AI', they mean Deep Learning specifically вҖ” convolutional neural networks processing camera feeds. When a bank's fraud system uses 'ML', it might mean a simpler model like a gradient boosted tree, not a neural network at all. Precise language matters in job interviews and project design.
explain_back_prompt: Draw the three nested circles from memory and explain what makes each one different. Give a real example of something that is ML but NOT deep learning.

build_task.title: Categorise 10 AI products correctly
build_task.context: Precision about what type of AI you're dealing with shapes how you build and debug it.
steps: List 10 AI-powered products or features you know of
For each, decide: is it rule-based AI, ML, or Deep Learning?
Research 2 of them to verify вҖ” look for 'how it works' blog posts or technical papers
Find one example that surprised you
Write one sentence explaining why the distinction matters for each
build_task.expected_output: A categorised list of 10 products with your reasoning and any corrections from research.
build_task.hint: Recommendation systems at Netflix and Spotify use Deep Learning. Excel's 'flash fill' uses a simpler rule-learning approach. Spam filters can be either.
